comments.body: Exactly. You need to bind the arguments to advice method parameters. You actually do not need argNames, they are optional. Order and types of arguments are more important.

answers.body:
It turned out that to solve this one must specify && args() within pointcut expression:

@Around(value = "execution(* t.s.l.LeaderboardService.getTopScores(..)) && args(tournamentId, limit)", argNames = "tournamentId,limit")
public Object checkMDC(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp, Long tournamentId, int limit) throws Throwable {
  // use tournamentId and limit
  return forTournament(pjp);
}

title: Pointcut does not match target method if argNames are specified
body:
I have an interface with implementation class being a Spring bean:

public interface LeaderboardService {
    List<PlayerRankWithDetails> getTopScores(Long tournamentId, int limit);
}

For this I've created an aspect:

@Aspect
public class LeaderBoardServiceMDCAspect {
    
    @Around(value = "execution(* t.s.l.LeaderboardService.getTopScores(..))")
    public Object checkMDC(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp) throws Throwable {
        return forTournament(pjp);
    }
}

This works fine, but as soon as I specify argNames within @Around in order to have invocation arguments injected into my pointcut method as demonstrated in spec

@Aspect
public class LeaderBoardServiceMDCAspect {
    
    @Around(value = "execution(* t.s.l.LeaderboardService.getTopScores(..))", argNames = "tournamentId,limit")
    public Object checkMDC(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp, Long tournamentId, int limit) throws Throwable {
        return forTournament(pjp);
    }
}

the pointcut suddenly stops matching target method (e.g. if I put a breakpoint on the line return forTournament(pjp); debugger won't stop there). What am I doing wrong?
